page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processors, which translate to 16 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This configuration does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in specific compute and memory tasks. The instance achieves top-tier memory performance, ranking in the top 10% for PassMark memory scores, and demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests. Cryptographic operations and physics simulations are also highly efficient, scoring in the top 25% on PassMark encryption and physics tests. For machine learning workloads, the server delivers top-tier text generation speeds for small language models and strong performance for medium-sized models, though it lacks the capacity for larger models due to memory constraints.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c2d-highcpu-32 offers a dense compute profile that minimizes memory overhead costs for workloads that do not require large RAM footprints. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs further streamlines the cost structure for pure CPU-bound tasks. This makes the instance highly suitable for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, image processing, physics simulations, static web serving, and lightweight language model inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processors on the x86_64 architecture. Lacking bundled local storage and GPUs, this instance is tailored for CPU-heavy tasks. It delivers strong single-core performance and top-tier memory scores, alongside highly efficient cryptographic and physics simulation capabilities. The 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU ratio provides a cost-effective hardware profile for resource-dense compute workloads. It is well-suited for software compilation, image processing, static web serving, and small-to-medium language model text generation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, delivering strong single-core and top-tier memory benchmark performance.

tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and a dense memory-to-vCPU ratio for CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processors
64.0 GB of system memory with top-tier memory benchmark scores
Strong single-core CPU, encryption, and physics simulation performance
Optimized for CPU-bound tasks without the cost overhead of bundled storage or GPUs
Best suited for software compilation, image processing, and lightweight LLM inference
